goals.g1: Correct the earlier mischaracterization: build a minimal plain-PerspectiveGraph-compatible LayeredGraph stub (materialize, derive_roster, roster, set_roster, edges_of, set_edges, adopt_node against a single throwaway base layer) and reuse the REAL apply_compound unchanged, replacing the hand-rolled reverse_footprint_lightweight/reconstruction logic with the proven mechanism.

prep_note: Sven caught that I read too little of apply_compound before declaring it a blocker. Prepared before building the fix.

prior_candidates.statement: ADOPTED: cand_bfs_direct_comparison, WITH the falsifier's correction incorporated: (1) build the candidate reconstruction via rebuild(result_graph.copy(), reversed_op.schema.edits, reversed_binding) -- reusing the proven mechanism, no new materialization logic. (2) Pick anchor from the CONFIRMED-unchanged set (not from an arbitrary match_all binding). (3) BFS-grow a correspondence from the anchor: for INHERITED/confirmed-unchanged real nodes, compare incident edges by DIRECT real-id equality (same physical node, by construction). For BORN/reconstructed nodes (freshly minted, no shared id with base_graph possible), compare STRUCTURALLY instead -- find whether some node in base_graph, adjacent to the already-confirmed correspondence, has matching edge structure relative to what's already anchored; require this structural match, not id equality, for born positions specifically. (4) Require full coverage: every node in both the reconstruction and base_graph must be reached by the BFS; unreached/disconnected nodes are a coverage gap, not a silent pass. Rejected: cand_seeded_match_all (failed its falsifier -- derive_match_view requires graph2-specific scaffolding a plain materialized graph doesn't have; a confirmed blocker, not an inconvenience).

derived_from.rule: adopt the candidate that survived its falsifier, incorporating the real correction it surfaced (born nodes need structural not id-based matching), and reject the one shown to have a concrete, confirmed blocker

goals: Implement the isomorphism check (refined_classifier's final, still-unbuilt piece): choose an anchor from a CONFIRMED-unchanged node (not from match_all's arbitrary resolution), use it to seed a structural correspondence, materialize ('build') the reconstructed candidate layer, and compare it against the retained previous layer (base_graph).

derived_from.rule: adopt the candidate that survived its falsifier, incorporating the real correction it surfaced (born nodes need structural not id-based matching), and reject the one shown to have a concrete, confirmed blocker (imported, parents not re-grounded — external provenance)

goals: Record the implemented check_isomorphism_anchored and its two-case test: positive case (genuine match) returns reversible:True with a confirmed anchor; negative case (deliberately corrupted retained layer) correctly returns reversible:False with the exact edge mismatch, confirming the check can genuinely fail, not just default to True.

prior_candidates.statement: After fixing the construction bug: fired rule B then rule A (read-before-write ordering, no apply_compound/redirect logic used) against a base graph with a genuine boundary_crossings overlap (rule B's crossing target R is inside rule A's own matched region). Result: forward firing correctly preserved the S->R edge this time. per_match_multiplicity = {0:1, 1:1} (no ambiguity). resolve_reverse_compound reported consistent:True (genuine overlap correctly matched between forward and reverse). reverse_footprint_lightweight + footprint_is_consistent also passed (both real nodes confirmed unchanged, correctly a subset of s_forward). This is the FIRST genuine multi-match compound test run today, and three of the four pipeline stages (collect, overlap, footprint) verified working correctly for it.

derived_from.rule: once the construction bug is fixed, the actual pipeline components (already written generically, no length-1 assumption in collect_reversed_matches or resolve_reverse_compound) work correctly against a real overlapping case

goals: Build a genuine two-rule overlapping compound test (not synthetic single-rule): two matches whose overlap is a real boundary_crossings case (one match's live crossing target is inside another match's own matched region), fired via two ordered rebuild() calls (read before write, avoiding the need for full apply_compound redirect logic). Run it through collect_reversed_matches and resolve_reverse_compound (both already written generically over matches:list, no length-1 assumption) to see whether they actually work for N>1, and determine what check_isomorphism_anchored needs to handle the N=2 case (it currently hard-gates on len(matches)==1).

prior_candidates.statement: Implemented in basic_machinery/reverse_compound.py: build_reversed_operation (wraps build_reversed_graph2+compile_schema, cached), collect_reversed_matches (phase 1, blind match_all per rule, flags per-rule multiplicity), resolve_reverse_compound (phase 2, OV_forward vs OV_reverse structural comparison via detect_overlaps reused unmodified twice -- no ordering, no enumeration, per this session's correction). Verified end-to-end against a single-rule synthetic case using an explicit-category rule. NOT verified: any genuine multi-rule compound firing (no test built); footprint_is_consistent is still not wired to a real apply_compound aggregate; the full isomorphism-to-retained-previous-layer check is not implemented at all -- resolve_reverse_compound only gets as far as the OV comparison. A real, uncorrected defect was found and left uncorrected (root_cause_and_rule_misapplied): reversed rules built from a forward rule using ph_all_four (blanket, variable-presence preservation) will incorrectly find zero matches against real firings where fewer than all 4 declared categories are actually present, because the exact-degree matcher cannot express 'however many of these are present'. This affects any real rule using ph_all_four (at least spine_finalise_v1.py's out_rspine, confirmed earlier this session) and is carried forward, not fixed.

derived_from.rule: state plainly what is verified working, what is unverified, and what is a known, real, uncorrected defect -- not overclaiming completeness

goals: Implement the corrected reversibility check design (per ers_lf6_repair.json and ers_compose_reversed_compound.json's surviving structure): collect_reversed_matches(matches, result_graph) -- per-rule blind match_all of each reversed rule against the result, flagging per-rule multiplicity; resolve_reverse_compound -- compare OV_forward (detect_overlaps on the known forward matches/base graph) against OV_reverse (detect_overlaps on the reverse matches/result graph), no enumeration, no ordering; reverse_fire -- thin orchestrator wiring these to footprint_is_consistent (existing) and the isomorphism check.

prior_candidates.statement: The anchor must come from the CONFIRMED-unchanged set (compute_unchanged_candidates + per-firing confirmed crossings, already built in reverse_footprint_lightweight) -- NOT from an arbitrary node that match_all's binding happens to include. A confirmed-unchanged node's real id is verified identical in both base_graph and result_graph BY CONSTRUCTION (it survived with provably no change), which is exactly what makes it usable as a zero-search, known-correct seed for growing a correspondence -- an arbitrary matched node has no such guarantee.
goals: Implement the isomorphism check (refined_classifier's final, still-unbuilt piece): choose an anchor from a CONFIRMED-unchanged node (not from match_all's arbitrary resolution), use it to seed a structural correspondence, materialize ('build') the reconstructed candidate layer, and compare it against the retained previous layer (base_graph).

own_error_confirmed.statement: chk_falsifier_dryrun (ers_checker_continuation.json) concluded 'no such helper exists... building one is real, unbuilt integration work' based on reading only apply_compound's signature and its first two lines (lg.materialize call). A fuller read this session (all of apply_compound, plus layers.py's derive_roster/set_edges/adopt_node) shows every LayeredGraph touchpoint is a trivial operation against a single roster/edge-dict: derive_roster = (roster-consumed)|born; edges_of/set_edges/adopt_node are simple per-node dict reads/writes. This was an insufficiently thorough read leading to a wrong conclusion, not a genuine blocker.

stub_design.statement: Build a minimal stub class implementing exactly the methods apply_compound calls: materialize(layer) returns the supplied plain PerspectiveGraph (ignoring layer key, single base layer only); roster/set_roster/derive_roster implemented per layers.py's own documented semantics; edges_of(node,layer) returns {e in base_graph.edges if e touches node}; set_edges/adopt_node record into simple dicts for later inspection (the reconstruction result). A minimal registry stub (whatever apply_compound's 'registry' parameter actually requires) is also needed -- check its usage before assuming a no-op stub suffices.

derived_from.rule: build the adapter directly from what the function actually calls, verified by reading, not assumed from the signature alone this time

stub_validated.statement: PlainGraphLayeredStub + SimpleRegistry built (compound_stub.py) and validated: apply_compound runs UNCHANGED against it for a genuine two-match case, producing correct output_maps/born/consumed/changed, and materialize_result correctly reconstructs the resulting graph (falling back to base_graph edges for unchanged nodes, matching delta_representation's convention). Fixed one bug found along the way in the stub itself (PerspectiveGraph has no adopt_node/pre-existing-id insertion method -- Node is a plain frozen dataclass, insert directly into the internal _nodes set).

cyclic_overlap_correctly_rejected.statement: Important side-finding: the FIRST two-rule test attempted had a genuinely CYCLIC mutual boundary-crossing (each rule's live crossing touched the OTHER rule's own matched node -- boundary_crossings showed BOTH (0,1) and (1,0) entries). apply_compound's real resolve_compound correctly detected this as an unresolvable redirect cycle and raised ValueError rather than silently producing a wrong result -- this is correct, safe behavior for a genuinely pathological construction, not a bug. The test had to be adjusted to a one-directional overlap (rule A declares the crossing for matching/degree purposes only, without preserving/grabbing it) to get a valid, resolvable compound case.

chk_falsifier_stub.method: does the stub genuinely reuse apply_compound's unmodified logic, or does it silently reimplement/duplicate any part of resolve_compound's redirect handling?
chk_falsifier_stub.result: Genuine reuse confirmed: apply_compound (schema.py) was called directly, unmodified, no copy/reimplementation of resolve_compound or its redirect logic anywhere in compound_stub.py. compound_stub.py only implements the LayeredGraph INTERFACE (materialize, roster, derive_roster, edges_of, set_edges, adopt_node) plus a convenience materialize_result helper for OUR OWN use (not part of what apply_compound calls) -- the actual compound-resolution logic is 100% the real, existing code.

chk_relevance_stub.method: connect the evidence to this session's stated goal
chk_relevance_stub.result: Goal: correct the earlier mischaracterization and build a minimal adapter reusing the real apply_compound. Directly reports the built, validated stub and the genuine reuse (not reimplementation) of apply_compound's logic.

committed.statement: PlainGraphLayeredStub + SimpleRegistry built (compound_stub.py) and validated: apply_compound runs UNCHANGED against it for a genuine two-match case, producing correct output_maps/born/consumed/changed, and materialize_result correctly reconstructs the resulting graph (falling back to base_graph edges for unchanged nodes, matching delta_representation's convention). Fixed one bug found along the way in the stub itself (PerspectiveGraph has no adopt_node/pre-existing-id insertion method -- Node is a plain frozen dataclass, insert directly into the internal _nodes set).
